label: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 6

extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus survived from early breakaway on coastal climb finale, holding off surging ROGLIČ by meters after crosswinds shredded peloton earlier.
tactical_patterns: Movistar isolated Elsen by pacing crosswind section at 28kph, forcing GC contenders to expend energy before final climb.
CORT conserved energy at intermediate sprint letting BOL take points, then attacked breakaway companions on final climb approach.
ROGLIČ sat protected through crosswinds while rivals chased, then attacked final meters with fresh legs nearly catching breakaway.
Ineos ran Ecuadorian domestique train for BERNAL up climb despite not being jersey defenders, revealing GC ambitions.

rider_signals.ELSEN Kenny: negative: lost red jersey in crosswinds, unable to follow Movistar's pace in windy section
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: dropped in crosswinds alongside Elsen, concerning weakness in non-mountain terrain for GC contender
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: minimal energy expenditure through crosswinds then surged past chasers in finale to take both time and jersey
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: animated chase on final climb despite being primarily sprint-oriented, unexpected climbing strength shown
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Ineos deployed full climbing train despite no jersey obligations, signaling they see him in winning form
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: Movistar's aggressive crosswind tactics put three riders within 41 seconds, justifying multi-leader strategy
rider_signals.BADGIOLI Davide: positive: followed Roglič's late surge to claim third, showing opportunistic positioning in reduced sprint
